task:
Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #2215: [Feature] Langfuse Cloud Prompt Migration + SDK Integration ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/2025slideheroes-sophie` 2. `git fetch upstream && git checkout upstream/dev && git checkout -b sophie/issue-2215` 3. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 2215 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes` 4. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 5. Run `pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck` before committing 6. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: [Feature] Langfuse Cloud Prompt Migration + SDK Integration (#2215)"` 7. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-2215` 8. Open PR via GraphQL (same-org fork — `gh pr create` doesn't work): ```bash gh api graphql -f query=' mutation CreatePR { createPullRequest(input: { repositoryId: "R_kgDON3X_Ow" baseRefName: "dev" headRefName: "sophie/issue-2215" headRepositoryId: "R_kgDORH2m4g" title: "feat: [Feature] Langfuse Cloud Prompt Migration + SDK Integration" body: "Closes #2215\n\nImplemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan." }) { pullRequest { url number } } }' ``` ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary - Integrate Langfuse SDK with lazy initialization and graceful degradation when not configured - Refactor prompt-manager.ts to fetch prompts from Langfuse Cloud with automatic fallback to existing local templates - Preserve the existing `{{variable}}` template syntax since Langfuse uses compatible Mustache-style templating - Add observability tracing that captures tokens, cost, and latency without disrupting existing Supabase usage tracking
Design Choices
Design Choice 1: Prompt Type Selection in Langfuse **Options Considered:** 1. Store all prompts as "text" type - simpler but loses message structure 2. Store prompts as "chat" type - preserves system/user role separation **Chosen Option:** 2 **Rationale:** Use Langfuse's "chat" prompt type to preserve the existing message structure and role separation, which aligns with how the codebase currently organizes prompts.
Design Choice 2: Prompt Naming Convention in Langfuse **Options Considered:** 1. Use ticket's naming (title-creator, audience-creator, ideas-creator, situation-improvements, text-simplifier, test-outline-creator) 2. Use existing template registry names (title-suggestions, audience-suggestions, test-outline) plus add missing ones **Chosen Option:** 1 **Rationale:** Use the ticket's naming convention for consistency with the feature specification, and update the local template registry mapping to align with these new names.
Design Choice 3: Caching Strategy **Options Considered:** 1. Implement custom in-memory caching with configurable TTL 2. Leverage Langfuse SDK's built-in caching (60s TTL, stale-while-revalidate) **Chosen Option:** 2 **Rationale:** Use Langfuse SDK's built-in caching mechanism, which implements stale-while-revalidate pattern and handles refresh automatically, avoiding custom caching code.
💡 User Tips Regenerate the plan with different choices with `@coderabbitai `. ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Langfuse SDK Foundation Establish the Langfuse SDK integration by adding the dependency, configuring environment variables with proper validation, and creating a client wrapper that follows existing codebase patterns.
Task 1: Add Langfuse SDK Dependency Add the Langfuse npm package to the ai-gateway package dependencies. - Update `packages/ai-gateway/package.json` to add `langfuse` as a production dependency - Ensure version compatibility with existing OpenAI SDK integration
Task 2: Configure Environment Variables Add Langfuse-specific environment variables with documentation and Zod-based validation following patterns established in billing packages. - Add `LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY`, `LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY`, and `LANGFUSE_HOST` entries to root `.env.example` with descriptive comments - Create `packages/ai-gateway/src/schema/langfuse-env.schema.ts` with Zod schema for validating Langfuse environment variables - Schema should make variables optional to support fallback-only mode when Langfuse is not configured - Include `.describe()` annotations for documentation
Task 3: Create Langfuse Client Wrapper Create a client initialization module that handles Langfuse SDK setup with proper configuration and graceful degradation. - Create `packages/ai-gateway/src/langfuse/langfuse-client.ts` for client initialization - Implement lazy initialization pattern (create client on first use) - Include helper function `isLangfuseConfigured()` to check if environment variables are set - Follow the conditional initialization pattern from `gateway-client.ts` for consistency - Export client getter function and configuration check utility
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` Establish Langfuse SDK integration foundation with dependency management, environment configuration, and client initialization. The goal is to set up Langfuse SDK with proper validation and graceful degradation patterns. **Add Langfuse SDK dependency:** - Add `langfuse` npm package to `packages/ai-gateway/package.json` as a production dependency - Ensure compatibility with existing OpenAI SDK **Configure environment variables:** - Add `LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY`, `LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY`, and `LANGFUSE_HOST` to root `.env.example` with descriptive comments - Create `packages/ai-gateway/src/schema/langfuse-env.schema.ts` with Zod schema - Make all variables optional to support fallback-only mode - Add `.describe()` annotations following existing billing package patterns **Create client wrapper:** - Create `packages/ai-gateway/src/langfuse/langfuse-client.ts` - Implement lazy initialization pattern (initialize on first use) - Add `isLangfuseConfigured()` helper to check environment variable presence - Follow conditional initialization pattern from `gateway-client.ts` - Export client getter function and configuration check utility ```
### Phase 2: Prompt Management Refactor Refactor the prompt management system to fetch prompts from Langfuse Cloud at runtime while preserving local templates as fallbacks for resilience.
Task 1: Create Langfuse Prompt Fetching Service Build a service layer that handles fetching prompts from Langfuse with proper error handling and type conversion. - Create `packages/ai-gateway/src/langfuse/prompt-service.ts` for Langfuse prompt operations - Implement `fetchPromptFromLangfuse(promptName: string)` function that returns `ChatMessage[]` format - Handle conversion from Langfuse chat prompt format to existing `ChatMessage` interface - Include error handling that logs failures using `createServiceLogger`
label: neo-issue-2215
